content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
August 15, 1989 
Laura M. Morgan 
California Interior Designers PAC 
11140 Fair Oaks Blvd., Suite 7 
Fair Oaks, CA 
95628 
Dear Ms. Morgan: 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-89-410 
You have requested advice on behalf of the California 
Interior Designers Political Action Committee (CIDPAC) concerning 
the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act of 1974. 1 
QUESTION 
Must the California Interior Designers PAC disclose 
contributions made to candidates by members of the California 
Legislative Conference on Interior Design (CLCID) when CLCID 
provided the members a credit for dues in the amount of their 
contributions? 
CONCLUSION 
CIDPAC is only required to disclose contributions made from 
PAC funds and is not required to disclose contributions made 
directly to candidates by CLCID or its members. 
However, by 
providing the dues credit, CLCID essentially "reimbursed" its 
member organizations for their campaign contributions. 
Therefore, 
CLCID was the "true" source of the contributions, and the member 
organizations were the intermediaries for such contributions. 
FACTS 
CLCID is a coalition of members of the California chapters of 
various groups related to interior designing interests. 
During 
the first six months of 1989, two California chapters of an 
organization made contributions to political candidates. 
In turn, 
CLCID gave the chapters "credit for dues" assessments. 
CLCID does not have authority or control over individual 
chapters or their treasuries. 
However, the contributions in 
question were made at the suggestion of CLCID. 
I 
Government Code Sections 81000-91015. 
All statutory 
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. 
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations 
Section 18000, et seq. 
All references to regulations are to Title 
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660 


Laura M. Morgan 
Page Two 
On August 7, 1989, you advised me that CLCID has not made 
contributions in the past except in support of CIDPAC. 
ANALYSIS 
Under Section 84302, a person who makes a contribution on 
behalf of another is an intermediary for that contribution and 
both the intermediary and the "true" contributor must be reported 
by the recipient. 
Since the member organizations made political 
contributions at the suggestion of CLCID, and CLCID in turn 
reduced the members' dues obligation in an amount equal to the 
contributions made, CLCID must notify the recipient that it was 
the contributor of those contributions and that the member 
organizations were acting as intermediaries. 
In addition, CLCID may become a recipient committee under 
Regulation 18215 in the next calendar year in which it makes 
contributions totalling $1,000. 
See the enclosure entitled 
"Organizations Which Receive Funds for Purposes Other Than Making 
contributions to or Expenditures on Behalf of California State and 
Local Candidates, Ballot Measures or committees," page two, 
sUbsection A. 2. 
If you have additional questions, please contact me at (916) 
322-5662. 
By: 
Enclosure 
Sincerely, 
Kathryn E. Donovan

RE: 
Amend 2 Cal. Code of Regulations Section 18428 
Dear Ms. Donovan: 
In an attempt to interpret the above referenced amendment 
dated 6/8/89 for preparation of the Semi-Annual Report, Form 
420, for 
the 
California 
Interior 
Designers 
PAC, 
we 
respectfully request clarification of the following in order 
to file our report correctly and on time: 
1. 
The California Legislative Conference on Interior Design 
is a coalition of members of the California chapters of 
The American 
Society 
of 
Interior 
Designers, 
The 
Institute of 
Business 
Designers, 
The 
International 
Furnishings & 
Design 
Association, 
The 
International 
Society of 
Interior 
Designers 
and 
Independent 
Professional Interior Designers. 
These members pay 
annual dues to CLCID to support its activities. 
2. 
Dur ing the first six months of 1989, 
two California 
chapters of 
a 
professional 
organization 
made 
contributions to political candidates 
and, 
in 
turn, 
CLCID gave the chapters credit for dues assessments. 
Al though CLCID 
has 
no 
au thor i ty 
or 
control 
over 
individual chapters 
or 
their 
treasury, 
these 
contributions were made at its suggestion. 
Given the above circumstances, the wording of the amendment 
is not precisely clear as to whether or not the contributions 
by these two chapters would have to be reported on Form 420 
by CIDPAC. 
Because of 
the 
reporting 
deadl ine, 
we 
would 
be 
most 
appreciative of an early decision concerning this matter. 
Thank yo"/u. 
~/ 
I~ 
Laura M. Morgan, Treasurer

California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
July 14, 1989 
Laura M. Morgan 
California Interior Designers PAC 
11140 Fair Oaks Boulevard, suite 7 
Fair Oaks, CA 
95628 
Re: 
Letter No. 89-410 
Dear Ms. Morgan: 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act 
was received on July 11, 1989 by the Fair Political Practices 
commission. 
If you have any questions about your advice request, 
you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5662. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or 
more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21 
working days if your request seeks formal written advice. 
If more 
information is needed, the person assigned to prepare a response 
to your request will contact you shortly to advise you as to the 
information needed. 
If your request is for informal assistance, 
we will answer it as quickly as we can. 
(See Commission 
Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 18329).) 
You also should be aware that your letter and our response 
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon 
receipt of a proper request for disclosure. 
JP:plh 
~eanne Pritchard 
Chief Technical Assistance 
and Analysis Division

428 T Street. Suite 800 • 
P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 9S804~0807 • (916) 322~S660
